meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Stop Debugging Hell
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn to use SaaS starter kits, Antigravity, and Claude Code to ship faster and impress clients with professional results.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing bugs by using a pre-configured SaaS starter kit. By leveraging standardized architectures with Antigravity and Claude Code, you eliminate common configuration errors and boilerplate traps. This shift allows you to focus on high-value business logic while AI tools handle the structural integrity of your codebase automatically.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and you’re buried under a mountain of cryptic stack traces. We’ve all been there—staring at a screen at 2 AM, debugging an environment variable issue that shouldn't exist. It’s the silent killer of freelance profitability, turning a high-margin project into a bottomless pit of unpaid hours.

But the best developers don't write more code; they write less. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you transform your workflow from 'scrambling to fix' to 'shipping to win.' This tutorial shows you how to leverage AI-native tools to bypass the drudgery and deliver professional-grade software at lightning speed.

steps.title: Select a Hardened SaaS Boilerplate
steps.description: Don't reinvent the wheel. Choose a starter kit with built-in auth, database schemas, and billing. This provides a stable foundation where bugs are already squashed.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this project structure and identify the most stable, production-ready SaaS boilerplate that includes Stripe integration, Next.js, and Supabase.

steps.title: Automated Environment Scaffolding
steps.description: Environment variables are the leading cause of 'it works on my machine' syndrome. Use Antigravity to generate and validate your .env files instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure .env.example file based on the boilerplate dependencies, including placeholders for production keys and development mocks.

steps.title: Contextual Code Analysis
steps.description: Instead of manual debugging, feed your code context to Claude Code. It views the entire dependency tree to identify root causes of failures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current project for circular dependencies and common configuration anti-patterns that lead to runtime errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze the current codebase and identify potential race conditions in the authentication flow.'

steps.title: Automated Bug Reproduction
steps.description: Use AI to write the test cases that catch bugs before they reach the browser. This ensures your client's project remains bulletproof.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Playwright test script that simulates a user sign-up flow and asserts that all database records are correctly created.

steps.title: Continuous Deployment Integration
steps.description: Connect your repo to a CI/CD pipeline. By automating your deployment, you ensure that no broken code ever hits production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a GitHub Action workflow that runs linting, type-checking, and unit tests on every pull request.

pro_tips: Always use a 'shadow' environment variable file to prevent sensitive secrets from being committed to version control.
Force AI tools to act as a Senior QA engineer; ask them to 'find the most fragile part of this function' rather than just 'fix it'.
Standardize your folder structure across all client projects so your AI agents can navigate your codebase with zero friction.
Implement 'feature flags' early, allowing you to deploy broken code to a hidden state rather than crashing the production app.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to store your project-specific coding standards, ensuring consistent output across every file.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is running, focus on building your first custom feature using the same AI-driven workflow. Repeat the process for your next three client projects to build your own private library of reusable components.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop debugging and start building. Scale your freelance business by shipping faster than the competition. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
